full_text:
Intense heatwave: School holiday declared in these districts of Odisha
District Collectors of Kalahandi and Subarnapur declared closure of all schools and Anganwadi centres from tomorrow in view of the intense and unbearable heatwave conditions.
Bhubaneswar: District Collectors of Kalahandi and Subarnapur declared closure of all schools and Anganwadi centres from tomorrow in view of the intense and unbearable heatwave conditions.
In view of the prevailing heat wave condition and Red-Warning in respect of Kalahandi District issued by the IMD, Bhubaneswar on 17.04.2026 and keeping in view of the health of students, children, teachers and staff, the Schools and Anganwadi Centres (AWCs) of Kalahandi District will remain closed from 18.04.2026 to 21.04.2026, said the office of the Kalahandi District collector adding that the following instructions must be strictly adhered to in this regard;
- This order shall be applicable to all Govt., Govt. Aided, Private and all types of Elementary/Secondary/Higher Secondary Schools/OAVs/KVs and Anganwadi Centres (AWCs) of Kalahandi District.
- The Supplementary Exam. of Class-V and VIII of the schools shall be conducted from 7.00 AM to 9.00 AM. The Examinee students may be provided with alternative food like Fruits/Biscuits as per the prescribed guidelines.
- The Census Work as assigned shall be continued with taking all precautions.
Likewise, the Subarnapur district administration declared holiday for the students of Anganwadi centres, schools, colleges, and technical institutions from tomorrow till April 22, 2026 in order to protect the students and staff from the current severe heat conditions.

title: Rain brings relief amid heatwave in Rajasthan, UP: Delhi records 10°C temp drop, Odisha sizzles above 44°C,... - Bhaskar English
full_text:
Banda hottest in Asia as mercury hits 45.4°C:Heatwave alert in 10 states, including MP, C’garh, over next 4 days; schools shut in parts of Odisha
- Copy link
Severe heat has gripped almost all states across the country. Banda in Uttar Pradesh recorded a temperature of 45.4°C on Friday, the highest so far this summer not only in India but also across Asia.
This is 4.9°C above normal. Interestingly, just a week earlier on 9-10 April, Banda had recorded the lowest temperature in the plains of the country at 12°C.
From today onwards, heatwave conditions are likely over the next four days in Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, Jharkhand, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu and Kerala.
Rainfall brought some relief to Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana and Delhi on Friday, although temperatures in these regions still remained above 40°C.
In Uttar Pradesh, temperatures crossed 40°C in 16 districts, triggering heatwave conditions. Severe heat continues to affect around 50 cities, including Prayagraj and Varanasi.
Temperatures have also crossed 43°C in several districts of Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Odisha. School timings have been revised in 13 districts of Madhya Pradesh.
In Odisha, all schools, colleges and anganwadi centres will remain closed until 21 April in Bolangir, Subarnapur and Kalahandi, while Chhattisgarh has not announced similar closures.

How will the weather be in the next 2 days?
April 19:
A heat wave alert has been issued for parts of Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, and Maharashtra. Hot and humid weather will prevail in Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, West Bengal, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu.
Heavy rain is expected in Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, and Meghalaya. Thunderstorms and lightning are also possible in Jammu and Kashmir and Uttarakhand.
April 20:
Heatwave conditions are likely in Jharkhand and Odisha, while coastal areas will experience humid heat. Hot nights are likely in some parts of Odisha.
Heavy rain is possible in Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, and Meghalaya. Thunderstorms are also possible in Karnataka, Maharashtra, Chhattisgarh, West Bengal, Sikkim, and Telangana.
Weather news from states…
Madhya Pradesh: Khajuraho records 43.2°C, heatwave alert in 16 districts today
A severe heat wave has begun in the state. On Friday, the temperature reached 43.2 degrees Celsius for the first time this season, recorded in Khajuraho, Chhatarpur. Meanwhile, temperatures exceeded 42 degrees Celsius in nine cities. A heat wave warning was issued for 16 districts on Saturday. In light of the heat wave, school timings have been changed in 12 districts.
Uttar Pradesh: School timings changed in Kashi, mercury crosses 40 degrees in 17 districts
Uttar Pradesh is experiencing intense heat. School timings up to eighth grade in Varanasi have been changed due to the heat. Temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius were recorded in 17 districts, including Prayagraj and Jhansi. Temperatures will rise by 2-3 degrees over the next three days.
Chhattisgarh: Rajnandgaon reaches 44°C, 4 cities cross 42°C; two-day heatwave alert
The highest temperature in the state was recorded at 44°C in Rajnandgaon. There is no hope of relief from the heat in the next two days. The Meteorological Department has warned that heatwaves may occur in some areas of Raipur, Bilaspur, and Durg divisions over the next two days. The mercury has surpassed 42 degrees in four cities.
